hero.label: Knowledge Graphs
hero.body: Design complex data systems. Manage and migrate them seamlessly. Keep your application development quick, flexible, and sovereign.
hero.comingSoon.docs: Documentation is on its way. Stay tuned!
hero.comingSoon.getStarted: We're not quite ready yet — but we'd love to hear from you! Drop us a message and we'll keep you posted.
engine.label: Core Capabilities
engine.title: DomainMesh
engine.desc: Define schemas, validate structures, and manage the evolution of your knowledge graph with code-driven precision.
engine.schema.title: Schema Design
engine.schema.desc: Declaratively define your domain model using intuitive YAML configurations. Establish strong typed nodes and relationships.
engine.schema.detail:
Schemas in DomainMesh define nodes, fields, and edges — the building blocks of your knowledge graph. Both the schema itself and all instance data live in ArangoDB, because the schema IS a graph (inheritance hierarchies, edge declarations between node types).

Key capabilities:
• Support for abstract nodes and concrete inheritance hierarchies
• Field types include string, integer, decimal, date, boolean, enum, and file attachments
• Edge declarations define allowed relationships between node types with cardinality constraints
• Full internationalization — define labels and descriptions in as many languages as your domain requires
• Schema changes are versioned and tracked through event sourcing
engine.validation.title: Strict Validation
engine.validation.desc: Ensure data integrity with compiled validators that enforce constraints before any data touches your graph database.
engine.validation.detail:
The Rule Engine evaluates validation and business logic declaratively, before any mutation reaches the database. Rules are stored as part of your knowledge graph in ArangoDB and evaluated at runtime.

Validation layers:
• Field-level constraints: required, min/max, regex patterns, enum membership
• Node-level rules: cross-field validation, conditional requirements
• Edge-level rules: cardinality enforcement, referential integrity
• Errors returned to the frontend use i18n message keys — translation happens in the client, never on the server
engine.version.title: Version Control
engine.version.desc: Track schema evolution over time. Roll back changes and manage migrations automatically with built-in versioning.
engine.version.detail:
Every mutation in DomainMesh produces an event that is stored in a PostgreSQL event store — a complete, append-only, replayable record of how your domain has evolved.

This event sourcing architecture provides:
• Full audit trail of every schema and instance change
• Point-in-time reconstruction of any previous state
• Event-driven workflow triggers — when a form is submitted, it produces a workflow event that the Event Listener picks up
• Foundation for CQRS patterns and read-model projections
engine.go.title: Go Native
engine.go.desc: Built entirely in Go for extreme performance and minimal resource footprint in containerized environments.
engine.go.detail:
The entire backend is written in Go, taking advantage of its compiled performance, lightweight goroutines, and excellent tooling for cloud-native systems.

Architecture highlights:
• Microservice architecture with gRPC for internal service-to-service communication
• REST gateway for external/public-facing API access
• Structured, leveled logging via a dedicated logger package — every service initializes a named logger
• Deployed on Kubernetes with Helm charts for consistent, reproducible deployments on any cloud provider
• All secrets managed via cloud-native secret management — nothing stored in files or committed to git
engine.graphDb.title: ArangoDB Integration
engine.graphDb.desc: Powered by ArangoDB — a multi-model database combining graph, document, and key-value storage with the powerful AQL query language.
engine.graphDb.detail:
ArangoDB is the primary store for both schema definitions and instance data. This is a deliberate design decision — representing schemas as a graph enables powerful queries over inheritance chains, edge declarations, and field relationships.

Integration details:
• Schema graph: node types, field schemas, edge declarations, and inheritance hierarchies
• Instance graph: concrete nodes, field values, and edge instances
• Multi-model advantage: graph, document, and key-value access patterns in a single database
• Interface-based adapter pattern — the graphstore package abstracts ArangoDB behind a clean Go interface
• Open-source database with European roots (Germany) — no proprietary vendor lock-in
engine.api.title: API Generation
engine.api.desc: Automatically generate strongly-typed APIs (REST and GraphQL) based directly on your domain model schema.
engine.api.detail:
DomainMesh exposes your knowledge graph through automatically generated APIs. The external API layer uses REST for public-facing access, while internal service communication uses gRPC for type safety and performance.

API capabilities:
• CRUD operations for nodes, edges, and field values — all derived from your schema
• Schema Manager, Node Manager, Edge Manager, and File Manager services handle domain operations
• Tenant isolation built into every API call — multi-tenant by design
• File Manager handles uploads with MIME type checking and virus scanning
engine.mcp.title: AI-Assisted Modelling
engine.mcp.desc: An MCP server enables AI assistants to design and iterate on schemas conversationally — accessible to business analysts and engineers alike, no prior platform knowledge needed.
engine.mcp.detail:
DomainMesh exposes its schema design capabilities through a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, making it a first-class tool for AI-assisted development.

MCP capabilities:
• AI assistants can create, modify, and validate schemas conversationally
• Business analysts can describe their domain in natural language and get a validated graph schema
• Engineers without prior platform knowledge can onboard instantly through AI guidance
• All AI-generated changes go through the same validation pipeline — no shortcuts
• Full event sourcing audit trail of every AI-assisted modification
workflow.label: Orchestration
workflow.title: Workflow
workflow.desc: Control how your domain evolves with processes, automations, and human interactions — all governed by your schema.
workflow.bpmn.title: BPMN-Like
workflow.bpmn.desc: Follows BPMN-inspired types for quick learning and intuitive process design.
workflow.bpmn.detail:
The workflow engine uses BPMN-inspired activity types so teams familiar with business process modeling can get started quickly. Under the hood, orchestration is powered by Temporal — a battle-tested durable execution engine.

Workflow architecture:
• Workflow Orchestrator: a long-running Temporal process that reads the workflow template and executes activities in sequence
• Activity Workers: standard Go code running on Kubernetes that performs individual workflow steps
• Supports human tasks (approvals, form submissions), automations, and compensations
• Workflow state is derived by reading the graph — no separate mutable state to manage
workflow.api.title: API First
workflow.api.desc: Trigger, monitor, and control workflows programmatically through APIs.
workflow.api.detail:
Every aspect of the workflow engine is accessible through APIs. The Workflow Manager service handles template management and launching workflows, while Temporal provides durable execution guarantees.

API operations:
• Create and update workflow templates programmatically
• Launch workflow instances with parameters
• Query workflow status, history, and pending tasks
• Signal running workflows to advance or cancel
• All operations are tenant-scoped and permission-checked through the Authorization service
workflow.ai.title: AI Integration
workflow.ai.desc: Let AI participate in workflows safely — powered by the MCP server, within your schema and governance rules.
workflow.ai.detail:
AI activities can be embedded as workflow steps alongside human tasks and automations. The MCP server provides the bridge for AI assistants to interact with the domain model, while the schema validation and rule engine ensure governance and auditability.

AI workflow patterns:
• AI-assisted data extraction and classification into your knowledge graph
• Automated review and enrichment of node data with human-in-the-loop approval
• Schema-constrained AI outputs — the model can only produce data that passes your validation rules
• MCP-powered conversational interaction with the domain model
• Full event sourcing audit trail of every AI-generated change
workflow.integrate.title: Integrate Anything
workflow.integrate.desc: Connect to any external system or service through simple HTTP calls.
workflow.integrate.detail:
Activity workers can call any external system as part of a workflow step. Because workers are just Go code running on Kubernetes, integration is as simple as making HTTP calls — no special SDKs or adapters required.

Integration capabilities:
• REST and webhook integrations with automatic retry and timeout handling
• OAuth2, API key, and mTLS authentication support
• Event-driven triggers — external systems can push events into NATS JetStream to start workflows
• Temporal's durable execution guarantees mean failed integrations automatically retry with exponential backoff
workflow.async.title: Async Tasks
workflow.async.desc: NATS-powered message-driven architecture for resilient, scalable task management.
workflow.async.detail:
NATS JetStream serves as the event bus for real-time event streaming between services. The Event Listener watches for domain events and triggers the appropriate workflow activities.

Async architecture:
• NATS JetStream provides at-least-once delivery with message replay capabilities
• Events flow from domain mutations → Event Bus → Event Store (PostgreSQL) for persistence
• Event Listener picks up domain events (e.g., form submitted) and signals the Workflow Orchestrator
• Decoupled architecture — services communicate through events, not direct calls
workflow.yaml.title: Declarative YAML
workflow.yaml.desc: Describe your workflows in clean, human-readable YAML files.
workflow.yaml.detail:
Workflow templates are defined in YAML, making them version-controllable, diffable, and reviewable in pull requests. The declarative format describes what should happen, not how — the engine handles execution details.

YAML workflows support:
• Sequential and parallel activity execution
• Conditional branching based on node data or field values
• Human task definitions with form schemas and assignment rules
• Timer-based escalations and deadline handling
• Compensation activities for rollback on failure
footer.cta: Questions? Reach out anytime, we're here.
product.label: Product Overview
product.title: The Engine of
product.subtitle: High-level features and value proposition of the knowledge graph engine designed to connect your data sources to powerful applications.
product.features.title: Detailed Feature Breakdown
product.features.desc: See how DomainMesh fits seamlessly between your diverse data sources and intelligent applications, creating a unified knowledge graph.
product.graphEngine.title: Core Graph Engine
product.graphEngine.desc: A robust, highly scalable engine specifically designed for rapid knowledge graph generation and traversal. Process millions of nodes and edges with minimal latency.
product.graphEngine.detail:
The Core Graph Engine stores both schema definitions and instance data in ArangoDB — a multi-model database combining graph, document, and key-value capabilities. This is a deliberate design decision — the schema itself IS a graph (inheritance hierarchies, edge declarations between node types), making a graph database the natural home.

Engine architecture:
• Nodes, fields, and edges form the building blocks of your knowledge graph
• Support for abstract nodes with concrete inheritance hierarchies
• Field types: string, integer, decimal, date, boolean, enum, and file attachments
• Edge declarations define allowed relationships with cardinality constraints
• Interface-based adapter pattern — the graphstore package abstracts ArangoDB behind a clean Go interface
• Open-source database with no proprietary vendor lock-in
product.apiIntegration.title: API Integration
product.apiIntegration.desc: Seamlessly connect and integrate with existing systems via our comprehensive REST and GraphQL APIs. Extensible webhook system for real-time updates.
product.apiIntegration.detail:
DomainMesh exposes your knowledge graph through a dual-protocol API layer. REST serves as the public-facing gateway for tenants and external clients, while gRPC handles internal service-to-service communication for type safety and performance.

API architecture:
• Auto-generated CRUD operations for nodes, edges, and field values — all derived from your schema
• Dedicated service managers: Schema Manager, Node Manager, Edge Manager, File Manager
• Multi-tenant isolation built into every API call
• File Manager handles uploads with MIME type checking and virus scanning
• Event-driven webhooks via NATS JetStream for real-time notifications
product.dataOrchestration.title: Data Orchestration
product.dataOrchestration.desc: Orchestrate complex data flows efficiently. Built-in ETL capabilities allow you to ingest, transform, and map disparate data sources into a unified model.
product.dataOrchestration.detail:
Data orchestration is powered by Temporal — a battle-tested durable execution engine. The Workflow Orchestrator reads workflow templates and executes activities in sequence, with Activity Workers performing individual steps as standard Go code on Kubernetes.

Orchestration capabilities:
• BPMN-inspired workflow types for intuitive process design
• Supports human tasks (approvals, form submissions), automations, and compensations
• NATS JetStream event bus for real-time streaming between services
• Event Listener watches for domain events and triggers appropriate workflow activities
• Temporal's durable execution guarantees automatic retry with exponential backoff
product.schemaEvolution.title: Schema Evolution
product.schemaEvolution.desc: Evolve your domain model without downtime. Version-controlled migrations let you iterate rapidly while maintaining backwards compatibility.
product.schemaEvolution.detail:
Every mutation in DomainMesh produces an event stored in a PostgreSQL event store — a complete, append-only, replayable record of how your domain has evolved. This event sourcing architecture is the foundation for safe schema evolution.

Evolution features:
• Full audit trail of every schema and instance change
• Point-in-time reconstruction of any previous state
• Event-driven workflow triggers — schema changes can automatically kick off migration workflows
• Foundation for CQRS patterns and read-model projections
• All changes are tenant-scoped for safe multi-tenant operation
product.accessControl.title: Access Control
product.accessControl.desc: Fine-grained, graph-aware permissions. Control who can read, write, and traverse specific nodes, edges, and properties in your knowledge graph.
product.accessControl.detail:
The Authorization service provides RBAC-based permission checks across all API operations. Every request passes through permission evaluation before reaching the business logic layer.

Access control features:
• Role-based access control (RBAC) with tenant-scoped roles
• Permission checks integrated into every gRPC service call
• Graph-aware traversal permissions — control who can follow specific edge types
• User Manager handles authentication, user profiles, and session management
• All access decisions are logged in the event store for auditability
product.monitoring.title: Observability
product.monitoring.desc: Built-in metrics, tracing, and health checks. Monitor query performance, schema drift, and system health through standardized OpenTelemetry endpoints.
product.monitoring.detail:
Every service in DomainMesh initializes a named, structured logger with leveled output. Combined with the event sourcing architecture, you get complete visibility into system behavior.

Observability stack:
• Structured, leveled logging via a dedicated logger package — every service initializes a named logger
• Log level controlled per-service via the LOG_LEVEL environment variable
• Event store provides a complete audit trail of all domain mutations
• Health checks and readiness probes for Kubernetes deployment
• Kubernetes-native autoscaling and monitoring, compatible with any cloud provider
product.readyCta.title: Ready to experience the power of DomainMesh?
product.readyCta.desc: Join forward-thinking developers building the next generation of intelligent, context-aware applications. Start for free today.
tech.label: Under the Hood
tech.title: Built for
tech.subtitle: Deep dive into the core engine architecture powering DomainMesh. Engineered for scale, speed, and uncompromising flexibility.
tech.go.title: Go-Native Performance
tech.go.desc: Written from the ground up in Go, our engine leverages ultra-fast concurrent execution and optimized memory management. Experience sub-millisecond latencies and massively scalable throughput.
tech.event.title: Event-Driven Architecture
tech.event.desc: At the core is an immutable event log. Every state change is recorded as an event, enabling perfect auditability, point-in-time recovery, and seamless distributed stream processing.
tech.schema.title: Schema-Driven Flexibility
tech.schema.desc: Our dynamic knowledge graph adapts to your evolving business domain. Model complex relationships on the fly without rigid database migrations or downtime.
tech.arch.title: System Architecture
tech.arch.desc: A high-level view of the DomainMesh engine stack.
usecases.label: Industry Applications
usecases.title: Real-World
usecases.subtitle: Explore how knowledge graphs power next-generation solutions across industries.
usecases.finance.title: Finance
usecases.finance.desc: Fraud detection, risk assessment, and regulatory compliance through interconnected financial entities and transactions.
usecases.supply.title: Supply Chain
usecases.supply.desc: End-to-end visibility, bottleneck identification, and predictive maintenance across global supplier networks.
usecases.security.title: Cybersecurity
usecases.security.desc: Threat intelligence, vulnerability mapping, and attack surface management using relational graph structures.
usecases.ecommerce.title: E-commerce
usecases.ecommerce.desc: Personalized recommendations, inventory optimization, and dynamic pricing models based on complex user behavior.
usecases.healthcare.title: Healthcare
usecases.healthcare.desc: Patient data integration, drug interaction analysis, and clinical pathway optimization through connected medical knowledge.
usecases.manufacturing.title: Manufacturing
usecases.manufacturing.desc: Digital twins, production line optimization, and quality control through connected sensor and process data.
usecases.govtech.title: Public Sector
usecases.govtech.desc: Citizen data management, regulatory compliance tracking, and cross-departmental knowledge sharing with full data sovereignty and auditability.
usecases.legal.title: Legal & Compliance
usecases.legal.desc: Contract lifecycle management, regulatory obligation tracking, and compliance evidence gathering across complex legal frameworks and jurisdictions.
about.label: Our Story
about.title: Our Mission to
about.subtitle: Building the platform that powers the next generation of connected, intelligent data systems.
about.vision.title: The Vision
about.vision.desc: To empower European companies with sovereign, cloud-agnostic data infrastructure — bridging raw data and actionable domain models through AI-assisted design, open-source technology, and full control over where your data lives.
about.story.title: Our Story
about.story.desc: Born in Switzerland from a frustration with fragmented data models, rigid schemas, and dependence on overseas cloud platforms. DomainMesh is built on open-source technology, designed for European data sovereignty, and committed to giving organisations full control over their data infrastructure.
about.values.title: Core Values
about.values.subtitle: The principles that drive our engine forward.
about.scalability.title: Scalability
about.scalability.desc: Built to handle massive datasets without breaking a sweat. From single projects to enterprise-wide deployments, our architecture scales effortlessly.
about.speed.title: Speed
about.speed.desc: Real-time processing and sub-millisecond query latency are non-negotiable. We optimize every layer of the stack for maximum throughput.
about.simplicity.title: Simplicity
about.simplicity.desc: Complex underlying technology presented through clean, declarative YAML. We believe powerful tools should be accessible, not overwhelming.
about.sovereignty.title: Sovereignty
about.sovereignty.desc: Your data, your cloud, your rules. We believe European companies deserve infrastructure independence — not lock-in to overseas platforms.

privacy.dataCollected.item2: Technical data (IP address, browser type, device information)

privacy.purpose.item2: To improve our website and services
privacy.rights.title: Your Rights
privacy.rights.desc: Under the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (nDSG) and, where applicable, the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have the right to access, rectify, delete, or restrict the processing of your personal data. You may also object to data processing or request data portability.

cloud.label: Infrastructure
cloud.title: Cloud
cloud.desc: Deploy anywhere. Control where your data lives. Switch providers without rewriting a single line of code.
cloud.sovereignty.title: Data Sovereignty
cloud.sovereignty.desc: Choose exactly where your data resides — on any major cloud provider or European alternatives. Full compliance with Swiss (nDSG) and EU (GDPR) data protection regulations.
cloud.portable.title: Zero Lock-In
cloud.portable.desc: Built on Kubernetes and open-source technologies. Migrate between cloud providers with configuration changes, not code rewrites.
cloud.european.title: European Clouds
cloud.european.desc: First-class support for European cloud providers alongside GCP, AWS, and Azure. Your data stays where your regulations require it.
cloud.opensource.title: Open Source Stack
cloud.opensource.desc: ArangoDB, Temporal, NATS, Kubernetes — our entire infrastructure stack is open source. No proprietary dependencies, no vendor surprises.
